schema.title: tool / diagnostic procedure
schema.root_cause: what the tool reveals about the underlying issue
schema.fix: how to use it / what to fix based on output
schema.anomaly_classes: subset of {compaction, export, flush, full_cpu, full_memory, network_bandwidth2}
schema.iotdb_subsystems: subset of {Flush, Compaction, MemTable, TsFile, WAL, Memory, Network, Compression, Query, Cache, Procedure, Schema, GC, Election}

tuples.title: health_check.sh — preflight + runtime env audit
tuples.symptom: Node won't start; existing node behaves oddly (slow start, port conflicts, OOM during init, 'too many open files' during heavy write)
tuples.root_cause: Environment misconfiguration: blocked ports, JDK missing/wrong version, insufficient memory headroom, swap enabled, ulimit < 65535, missing write permission
fix: Run: health_check.sh -o all -ips <peer_ip> <peer_port1> <peer_port2>

Checks include: JDK ≥1.8, memory allocation per ConfigNode/DataNode, write perms on data/datanode/{data,consensus,system,wal} + ext/{pipe,udf,trigger}, free ports 16668/10730/11742/10750/10760/10710/10720, max open files ≥65535, swap disabled
Use -o local for own machine; -o remote with -ips to test peer connectivity
Output explicitly lists which check failed (e.g. 'Inaccessible server ports: 10.0.6.230:10311')

tuples.title: Fix 'too many open files' by raising ulimit to 65535
tuples.symptom: IoTDB logs throw 'Too many open files' or 'EMFILE'; new TsFile creation fails; query loaded files capped
tuples.root_cause: Default ulimit -n is 1024-4096 on most distros; IoTDB needs >=65535 because of many TsFile/WAL/consensus files per region
fix: Temporary: ulimit -n 65535
Permanent: append '* soft nofile 65535' and '* hard nofile 65535' to /etc/security/limits.conf
Re-login and verify with ulimit -n

tuples.title: Disable swap to prevent performance degradation
tuples.symptom: Random latency spikes; 'free -m' shows non-zero swap used; tail latencies far exceed averages
tuples.root_cause: IoTDB's JVM heap and memory-mapped files perform badly when pages get swapped to disk; OS swap competes with WAL/TsFile IO
fix: echo 'vm.swappiness = 0' >> /etc/sysctl.conf
swapoff -a && swapon -a
sysctl -p
Verify: free -m  → Swap line should be 0

tuples.title: print-iotdb-data-dir — visualise data directory layout
tuples.symptom: Suspect partition/region miscount; need to enumerate sequence/unsequence directories before manual surgery; suspected orphan directories
tuples.root_cause: Region/TimePartition folder structure can be skewed by failed migrations, deleted databases, or version upgrades
fix: tools/tsfile/print-iotdb-data-dir.sh <data_folder1[,folder2]> [output_path]
Output tree shows sequence/<database>/<region>/<time_partition> hierarchy
Cross-reference with SHOW REGIONS to verify physical layout matches metadata

tuples.title: print-tsfile (sketch) — inspect a single TsFile internals
tuples.symptom: Corrupted TsFile suspected; compaction fails on one file; need to verify Chunk/ChunkGroup integrity
tuples.root_cause: Crash during write may leave incomplete chunk headers or missing magic tail; sketch shows file truncation point
fix: tools/tsfile/print-tsfile-sketch.sh <tsfile_path> [output_path]
Look for: missing magic head (offset 0) / tail (END of TsFile), broken Chunk Group markers, ChunkIndex inconsistencies
If file is broken: move it aside, then START REPAIR DATA to let IoTDB reconstruct

tuples.title: print-tsfile-resource-files — verify .resource file matches its TsFile
tuples.symptom: Query returns inconsistent results; replica drift after migration; resource file timestamps differ from data file
tuples.root_cause: .resource sidecar stores device→time-range index for partition pruning; if it diverges from the actual TsFile, query plans go wrong
fix: tools/tsfile/print-tsfile-resource-files.sh <dir or .resource path>
Compare reported (device, start_time, end_time) with the actual TsFile's chunk timestamps from print-tsfile
Mismatch: delete the .resource file and restart DataNode (will regenerate) or run START REPAIR DATA
